extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2; rec_drug_b is set true only by r1. They target different outputs, so each output is resolved independently and priorities never make one suppress the other. r2 requires all 10 of its conjuncts true, one of which is prior_gi_bleed. r1 requires prior_gi_bleed. Therefore any input satisfying r2's full conjunction also satisfies r1's condition, so both rec_drug_a (via r2) and rec_drug_b (via r1) become true, violating mutual exclusion. The witness sets all 10 of r2's conjuncts true (which also fires r1); diabetes is false (rec_drug_c stays false by default, irrelevant to the property).
